because.text: First external acceptance-gate audit (Gemini 3.1 Pro High via agy, fed the recent diffs + both differential fuzzers). It found real defects the fuzzers had blind spots to. REAL, fixed: (1) Python verifier crashed AttributeError on a non-object signature entry -- s.get('actor') on a str; verify_store now guards non-list sigs + non-dict entries, and _iter_sigs() routes every settlement-path sig iteration through the same guard (Go was safe). (2) Python crashed RecursionError on deeply-nested JSON while Go returned bounded (Gemini had the direction inverted); all_records now catches RecursionError. (3) Fuzzer soundness: fuzz_differential Loop C used (cp>0)!=(cg>0), which silently PASSES a shared accept of a malformed record; strengthened to both-must-reject, which immediately exposed a no-op exp_num mutation in my own fuzzer. Added dirty cases (sigs_notlist, sig_scalar, deep_nest) + hostile.py named regressions. REFUTED empirically: ATP uint32 wrap in Go force (force cost <=3; R-S already uint64 with the check before narrowing; cost<=remaining always), scalar-record crash (handled), and 2 'missing' blocklist keys (accept 0/128 in Python AND Go -- rejected at decode); the 2 keys were blocklisted anyway as defense-in-depth. Lesson: external audit is the gate, self-review the substrate. cmd@v1 re-runs conformance PY+GO, selftest, the full suite, both fuzzer seeds, and sigma-conformance; transcript pinned. Filed in sigma-glyph as a sibling adjudication.

subject.note: Gemini 3.1 Pro external audit: fixed 2 verifier crashes (non-object sig; deep-nest) + a fuzzer blind spot (Loop C shared-accept); 3 findings refuted. Suite + fuzzers green.
